items.title: Quote of the day by Fred C. Kelly: "We may forgive ourselves for owning a market dullard when the rest of the market is also in the doldrums, but it is disheartening to see one’s favorite resting as quietly as a castor-oil bottle while the rest of the market goes gaily upward"
items.summary: Investors often face the agony of watching their chosen stocks lag while the broader market soars. This frustration can lead to costly mistakes like chasing rallies or abandoning sound investments. Fred C. Kelly's wisdom highlights that patience and conviction are crucial, distinguishing between temporary lulls and genuine business decline. This ambitious venture, to be developed in two phases by its subsidiary JSW Rayalaseema Steel Ltd, will initially focus on a 1 MTPA integrated plant for low-carbon steel.

items.title: JSW Rayalaseema Steel Plant will be completed in 2 yrs: AP CM Naidu
items.summary: Commercial production is targeted to commence by March 2028

items.title: Rs 403.40 Dividend, 1:3 Bonus, 1:10 Stock Split: 43 stocks to turn ex-date next week—FULL LIST
items.summary: Rs 403.40 Dividend, 1:3 Bonus, 1:10 Stock Split: The ex-date is important for investors because shareholders must own a stock before the ex-date to become eligible for dividends, bonus shares or stock split benefits. Investors buying shares on or after the ex-date will not qualify for the announced corporate action.

items.title: Rupee falls on-week as merchant, NDF-linked flows blunt dollar retreat
items.summary: The rupee fell nearly 1% this week, closing at 95.21 per dollar, slipping below the 95 mark for the first time in three weeks

items.title: Airport operators tell Centre airline fee relief no longer warranted
items.summary: Private airports are urging the civil aviation ministry to end the three-month discount on domestic flight landing and parking charges, set to expire July 6. The Association of Private Airport Operators (APAO) argues the geopolitical situation has stabilized, making the concession unnecessary. They propose allowing operators to recover foregone fees from airlines and suggest a mechanism for recouping these costs from revenue share payments.

items.title: SIF vs Mutual Funds: 7 key differences in strategy, risk, returns explained
items.summary: SIF vs Mutual Funds: Speaking to Zee Business, Harshvardhan Roongta, CEO, Roongta Securities, and Kirrtan Shah, Founder and CEO, Truvanta Wealth, explained how SIFs differ from mutual funds, who should invest in them, and why investors should avoid making decisions based solely on short-term market conditions.

items.title: Rupee closes at 95.21 vs USD, falls on-week as merchant, NDF-linked flows blunt dollar retreat
items.summary: The Indian rupee experienced a slight uptick on Friday, yet ended the week on a weaker footing due to overwhelming dollar demand driven by various market activities. Despite a rise in positive sentiment linked to a projected balance of payments surplus and new foreign inflows, pressures from maturing non-deliverable forward positions and large-scale merchant payments hampered its progress.

items.title: Food inflation reshaped household consumption in FY26, says Nestle India CMD
items.summary: Nestle India declared a special dividend of  ₹2 per equity share with a face value of  ₹1. Following the news, its shares rose over 1% on Friday

items.title: Maruti's plant inauguration by PMs of India, Japan 'greatest possible honour': T Suzuki
items.summary: Suzuki Motor Corporation President Toshihiro Suzuki expressed immense honor as Indian and Japanese Prime Ministers virtually inaugurated Maruti Suzuki's new Kharkhoda plant. This significant event underscores a deepened commitment to "Viksit Bharat," with a focus on human development. Suzuki emphasized this by visiting skill development and road safety institutes, highlighting the importance of nurturing talent for India's progress.

items.title: Nestle India sees strong growth opportunity in rural markets, bets big on deeper penetration: CMD
items.summary: Tiwary said Nestle's priority is to drive penetration-led volume growth

items.title: Food inflation shaped household choices; long-term growth potential intact: Nestle chairman
items.summary: Nestle India's Chairman highlighted how food inflation reshaped consumer choices, impacting pack sizes and purchase habits in FY26. Despite uneven demand and rising commodity costs, the company reported a significant revenue jump of 22.6% for the January-March 2026 quarter. Nestle India is now a global leader for Maggi and KitKat, emphasizing agility and consumer closeness for future growth.

items.title: India real estate attracts USD 2.9 billion in Q2 Calender Year 2026; Chennai, Bengaluru drive 27% inflows
items.summary: The report said domestic investments more than doubled to USD 1.33 billion during the quarter, accounting for 46 per cent of the total inflows. Foreign investments stood at USD 1.54 billion, contributing 54 per cent of the total despite global trade and capital deployment uncertainties arising from the West Asia crisis. The inflows were supported by select large transactions.

items.title: Maruti Suzuki gains after Kharkhoda plant launch; Rs 35,000 crore investment planned
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki shares opened at Rs 14,450 and touched an intraday high of Rs 14,520. The company's market capitalisation stood at around Rs 4.56 lakh crore.

items.title: Monsoon Update: Flash floods in multiple locations in Himachal Pradesh; orange alert issued in Mumbai
items.summary: Meanwhile, heavy rains in Mumbai disrupted normal life in Mumbai and created waterlogging at several places. As a result of it, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) upgraded its weather warning to an orange alert today, July 3.

items.title: Paytm's European arm secures payment institution licence in Luxembourg
items.summary: Paytm's European arm, Paytm Europe Payments, has secured a crucial payment institution license from Luxembourg's financial regulator, effective July 2, 2026. This approval allows the company to offer a range of payment services, including fund transfers and credit line-backed transactions. The move follows a significant investment by its parent company, One 97 Communications, to bolster the European entity's capital and support its business expansion.

items.title: Bajaj Housing Finance share price jumps 6% on strong Q1 update; is the stock still a buy?
items.summary: The stock has declined around 5 per cent so far in 2026, while the Nifty 50 has fallen about 7 per cent during the same period. Over the past one year, Bajaj Housing Finance has dropped 24 per cent, underperforming the benchmark index, which is down about 4.2 per cent.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on 3 July: Zensar Tech, HFCL, IndusInd Bank, Muthoot Finance, Lupin among top gainers
items.summary: The Indian stock market rose on July 3 before retreating as profit-taking emerged. The Nifty 50 closed up 0.40%, while the Sensex gained 0.35%. Realty led gains, but PSU Banks, Auto, and Metal sectors fell. Technology stocks showed signs of recovery amid easing rate hike concerns.

items.title: Stock market today: Sensex gains 260 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,250; IT, pharma shine
items.summary: Stock market today: The Sensex ended 262 points, or 0.34%, higher at 77,763.91, while the Nifty 50 rose by 95 points, or 0.39%, to end at 24,270.85.

items.title: Certificates of deposit issuances rise 38% in June amid tight liquidity, credit offtake
items.summary: When compared to the preceding May, the CD issuances jumped 61.79%

items.title: China-linked power firms get 2-year tender relief: Here's what it means for Indian businesses
items.summary: The move was largely expected by the market. As per analyst said the decision is unlikely to have a major impact on Indian power equipment companies such as BHEL, GE T&D India, and CG Power.

items.title: Bitcoin climbs above $61,000 as weak US jobs data fuels Fed rate cut hopes
items.summary: In the past 24 hours, Bitcoin was up 2.80% and Ethereum was up 6.24% to trade at $1,716 mark. Among the major altcoins, BNB, XRP, Solana, Tron, Hyperliquid, Dogecoin, and Cardano gained upto 6.68%.

items.title: IDFC First Bank Q1 update: Loan book grows 21% YoY to Rs 3.05 lakh crore; shares rise 2%
items.summary: IDFC First Bank reported strong provisional Q1 FY27 business growth, with loans and advances rising 20.6% year-on-year and deposits increasing 18%. CASA deposits and the CASA ratio also improved, reflecting healthy balance sheet expansion. The stock gained nearly 2% following the business update, while the bank maintained steady lending momentum.

items.title: Sensex holds 78,000, Nifty above 24,300 as IT stocks rally; HCL Tech jumps
items.summary: Softer-than-expected US jobs report cools expectations for a Fed rate hike in September, boosts risk appetite for emerging markets

items.title: Adani Enterprises surges near 52-week high after QIP launch and ₹1.08 lakh crore Aluminium JV announcement
items.summary: Traded volume stood at 16.62 lakh shares, with a traded value of ₹524.17 crore

items.title: Bank of Baroda stock falls over 3% after NMC settlement; should you buy the dip?
items.summary: PSU lender's stock extends losses after disclosing a US$600 million settlement in the NMC Health case despite reporting healthy Q1 business growth.

items.title: Strong El Niño may bring more extreme weather across the world, warns WMO
items.summary: From scorching heat to torrential rain, the weather development threatens another volatile weather season globally

items.title: Max Estates Q1 sales bookings jump five-fold to Rs 1,100 cr on strong housing demand
items.summary: Max Estates Ltd has reported a remarkable fivefold surge in sales bookings, reaching Rs 1,100 crore in the first quarter of this fiscal year. This impressive growth is attributed to robust demand for its housing projects across Delhi-NCR, particularly in Gurugram and Noida. The company also launched a new project in Gurugram with significant revenue potential, underscoring its strong market position and commitment to meeting buyer expectations.

items.title: Pragyawan Technologies files DRHP with Sebi for Rs 400 crore IPO
items.summary: Pragyawan Technologies has filed its DRHP with SEBI for an IPO comprising a Rs 400 crore fresh issue and an OFS of 15 million shares. The company will use fresh issue proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes, strengthening its diversified technology and infrastructure business.

items.title: Nifty heading to 25,000, Bank Nifty to 60,000 soon; prefer private banks, NBFCs over PSU banks: CA Rudramurthy BV
items.summary: Indian equity markets are showing strong upward momentum, breaking past key resistance levels. Analysts believe this rally has significant potential, with the Nifty eyeing 25,000. Supportive macro factors like stable crude prices, a steady rupee, and a slowdown in foreign investor selling are fueling optimism. The market favors private banks and NBFCs over PSU banks, with specific stock recommendations in pharma, IT, and platform businesses.

items.title: Can the ED attach a company’s assets after it enters insolvency? | Explained
items.summary: The legal question before the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal was whether the protection available under the IBC moratorium extends to assets that are simultaneously the subject of proceedings under the PMLA

items.title: Aastha Spintex IPO listing date next week. Here’s what GMP signals about share debut
items.summary: Aastha Spintex IPO was subscribed 3.30 times in total. The public issue was booked 1.27 times in the Retail Individual Investors category, and 6.03 times in the Non-Institutional Investors (NII) segment. The Qualified Institutional Buyers (QIB) category garnered 1.87 times subscription.

items.title: Auto demand remains strong, but OEM margins may stay under pressure in H1 FY27
items.summary: Indian auto sector anticipates strong demand across all vehicle types, though automakers may see profit margins squeezed in early FY27. Wholesale dispatches for passenger vehicles, commercial vehicles, and tractors remain robust, indicating sustained consumer interest. Premium segment demand is expected to hold steady, while mass-market sales might moderate later in the fiscal year.

items.title: Understanding consciousness: think of it like a neuro-hologram, one that AI can’t mimic
items.summary: Insights by researchers from the US and Sweden reveal that consciousness can be thought of as a kind of hologram projected by our brains—literally. This is not something every living being can do. Nor is it something AI is capable of.

items.title: EU's CBAM poses major compliance challenge for Indian MSMEs; Experts seek urgent government support
items.summary: Indian MSMEs are struggling with the EU's Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism, facing compliance hurdles due to a lack of financial and technical resources. Experts urge government intervention, technology transfer, and the development of alternative export markets to protect these smaller businesses. The current carbon accounting framework is seen as inequitable, disproportionately impacting developing economies and threatening the competitiveness of domestic steel and aluminium sectors.

items.title: Allcargo Global lists on NSE and BSE after demerger from Allcargo Logistics
items.summary: AGL houses the group’s international supply chain operations

items.title: Crude oil may drop to $60 as Hormuz shock fades away: Citi
items.summary: Global energy markets are rapidly getting back to normal

items.title: Kusumgar IPO price band fixed at Rs 398–419; Rs 650-crore issue opens July 8, GMP at 33%
items.summary: Kusumgar has set its IPO price band at Rs 398–419 for a Rs 650-crore offer for sale, opening July 8. The issue has a grey market premium of about 33%, signalling strong demand. The company has reported steady revenue and profit growth, supported by a diversified industrial fabric portfolio.

items.title: Why did CG Power, Hitachi Energy, Apar Industries and other capital goods stocks crash up to 10% today? Explained
items.summary: Capital goods firms, including GE Vernova T&amp;D India and Siemens Energy India, faced sharp declines after India's Centre allowed four Chinese manufacturers to join government tenders for power projects, impacting investor sentiment as tensions with China eased and infrastructure demands grew.

items.title: Explainer: Why govt plans to repeal Pharmacy Act 1948, and replace it with National Pharmacy Commission Bill
items.summary: The government plans to replace the Pharmacy Act, 1948 with a new National Pharmacy Commission Bill, 2026, creating a central commission, four specialised boards, and a mandatory exit exam for practice licenses.&nbsp;

items.title: Expert view: See compelling opportunities in financials, energy, metals, says Sandeep Neema of PL Asset Management
items.summary: Sandeep Neema of PL Asset Management uncovers promising investment sectors amidst market volatility. He advises against letting FII flow data dictate portfolio choices, emphasising a selective approach within financials, energy, and industrials to seize growth opportunities in India.&nbsp;

items.title: Knack Packaging IPO oversubscribed 25.75x on final day; NII category leads at 76.83x
items.summary: The Gujarat-based company manufactures printed and laminated woven polypropylene bags and serves customers across 68 countries

items.title: Market outlook: Monsoon, crude oil prices to earnings - Five key macro triggers for Dalal Street in H2CY26
items.summary: Market experts expect a more constructive outlook for Indian equities in H2CY26 due to falling crude oil prices and easing inflation. However, concerns about expensive valuations and an uncertain monsoon remain, potentially leading to modest returns despite a favorable macro environment.

items.title: India’s services PMI drops to 17-month low of 57.4 in June 2026 as demand softens
items.summary: The loss of momentum points to more challenging market conditions and weaker demand, particularly at home, says Pranjul Bhandari, Chief India Economist at HSBC

items.title: Cars24 scraps job titles and hierarchy in AI-era organisational overhaul
items.summary: Indian used-car platform Cars24 has ditched job titles and hierarchical ranks for all employees, adopting a single designation: 'Builder'. This radical restructuring, dubbed 'Flatland', aims to empower individuals based on ownership and impact, not formal position. The company believes this new model is essential for thriving in the AI era, fostering a culture where exceptional people collaborate to solve complex problems, ultimately boosting efficiency and profitability.

items.title: Restrictions on exporter meetings with DGFT officials could delay resolution of urgent trade issues: Report
items.summary: DGFT circular issued on May 11 says exporters should preferably meet officials of the rank of Additional Director or above to streamline interactions, bring transparency

items.title: Zydus Lifesciences ties up with Apollo Hospitals to offer Shield multi-cancer detection test
items.summary: Zydus Lifesciences has partnered with Apollo Hospitals to introduce the Shield multi-cancer detection test in India, made possible through an agreement with Guardant Health. This innovative blood test can identify signals for multiple cancer types in individuals aged 45 and above. The collaboration aims to enhance early cancer detection and improve patient outcomes across the nation, making advanced screening more accessible.

items.title: E20 causes ‘minor’ drop in vehicle mileage, Puri says, as India gears up for E100 fuel
items.summary: The Petroleum Minister acknowledged a minor drop in fuel efficiency with E20 petrol but highlighted improved acceleration and engine performance as key benefits. Despite consumer concerns about mileage and older vehicles, the government is pushing ahead with ethanol blending. India is also exploring higher blends like E25 and E85.

items.title: Sampark India Logistics IPO allotment date likely today; GMP, steps to check share allotment status of SME IPO
items.summary: Sampark India Logistics IPO allotment date is likely today, 3 July 2026. Investors can check Sampark India Logistics IPO allotment status online through the websites of BSE and the IPO registrar. Maashitla Securities is the Sampark India Logistics IPO registrar.

items.title: Flipkart ranked 5th best employer by Great Place To Work; underscoring a strong people-first culture
items.summary: Flipkart has secured a spot in India's Top 5 Best Companies to Work For 2026, a significant leap from its previous ranking. This recognition highlights the e-commerce giant's dedication to a people-first approach, emphasizing employee well-being, career development through internal mobility, and a performance-driven culture. The company's commitment to fostering innovation and providing inclusive benefits has been key to its success.

items.title: 'Don’t care’, says Donald Trump, chalking off alleged insider trading claims; $1.4 billion crypto windfall
items.summary: Donald Trump dismissed concerns over alleged insider trading and conflicts of interest after financial disclosures showed businesses linked to his family's cryptocurrency ventures generated an estimated $1.4 billion in 2025. Trump said he does not manage his businesses, calling the presidency a "much bigger purpose" than personal financial gains.

items.title: Today’s Gold Rate in India July 3: Gold prices up in Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Bengaluru, Ahmedabad
items.summary: 22, 24 carat Gold prices up across Indian cities on July 3

items.title: Marico's best volume growth in quarters isn't luck; Abneesh Roy on what's really driving it
items.summary: Marico is experiencing its strongest volume growth in quarters, signaling a genuine FMCG sector turnaround, not a statistical blip. Analyst Abneesh Roy anticipates this recovery to persist for at least two more quarters.  Marico's raw material costs, particularly copra, have significantly decreased, positioning the company for a substantial margin improvement. This, coupled with aggressive advertising, makes Marico a standout performer.

items.title: India has got a $5 trillion sleeping giant: Can govt wake it up?
items.summary: India holds an estimated $5 trillion worth of gold in households and temples, yet continues to spend billions importing more every year. As pressure on the rupee and trade deficit mounts, the government is considering reviving the Gold Monetisation Scheme by involving jewellers to unlock idle gold and turn dormant household wealth into productive economic capital.

items.title: 10 high-priced stocks rally up to 85% in CY26; here’s FII and MF exposure
items.summary: Despite a weak broader market, several premium BSE stocks priced above Rs 5,000 delivered strong CY26 gains, led by Hitachi Energy India, Apar Industries and Solar Industries India.

items.title: Earnings growth to stay robust at 14–16%; IT correction a buying opportunity: Vikas Khemani
items.summary: India's IT sector is poised for a comeback, with AI expected to boost, not hinder, growth.  Vikas Khemani of Carnelian Asset Management sees excessive pessimism, presenting an attractive long-term opportunity.  He highlights that historical tech shifts expanded the industry.  Stock selection is crucial, favoring early AI adopters.  Khemani also remains constructive on financials, power, and EV ancillaries, while cautious on value retail due to valuations.

items.title: Are AI deployment targets just another management fad? Ask Ford, Meta and Amazon
items.summary: The AI din is deafening as companies rush to gain from this wonder technology. Like other management fads before it, however, this one needs to be recognized for what it is. Amid all the cheering, listen closely to what early adopters are saying.

items.title: Home affordability holds firm despite rising prices: Knight Frank
items.summary: Homebuyer affordability remains strong across most Indian cities in early 2026, with lower borrowing costs offsetting rising property prices. Ahmedabad leads as the most affordable market, while Mumbai and NCR face affordability challenges. Despite price increases, healthy incomes and supportive financing continue to fuel residential demand, keeping sales near post-pandemic highs.

items.title: OpenAI discusses giving U.S. government 5% stake: Report
items.summary: Trump said on June 2 that he intended to meet top artificial intelligence firms to discuss the possibility of the government taking a stake in their companies to address public concern over AI

items.title: Market strategy: How should investors rebalance portfolios for H2CY26? Analysts favour banks, auto, metals, healthcare
items.summary: Market strategy: Valuations have also become more attractive. The Nifty 50 index is currently trading at a 12-month forward P/E ratio of 18.8x, around a 10% discount to its long-period average (LPA) of 21x. Its forward P/B ratio of 2.7x is about 5% below the historical average of 2.9x.

items.title: Tata Motors partners with Welspun Renewable Energy to develop 86 MW wind-solar hybrid project
items.summary: Tata Motors has joined forces with Welspun Renewable Energy to develop an 86 MW wind-solar hybrid project. This initiative will power four of Tata Motors' manufacturing plants in Jharkhand, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, and Karnataka with clean electricity. The project aims to generate 200 million units of green power annually, significantly reducing carbon emissions and supporting the company's sustainability goals.

items.title: Citi sees brent crude sliding to $60/bbl as supply fears ease
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions easing around the Strait of Hormuz are expected to drive Brent crude prices down to $60 a barrel by year-end, according to Citi. Global energy markets are normalizing, with shipping resuming and Gulf producers increasing output. Despite this, Chinese buyers remain absent, and inventories are lower than anticipated, suggesting a potentially uneven recovery. Other banks share this outlook, anticipating a return to market surplus.

items.title: Mukesh Ambani, Sunil Bharti Mittal among 40 founding members of global commission on AI: Details
items.summary: Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani and Bharti Enterprises Chairman Sunil Bharti Mittal have joined the AI for Good Global Commission, a new international body bringing together over 40 global leaders to promote responsible AI development.

items.title: Japan's Nikkei rises, ekes out weekly gain on Fed bets, domestic growth signs
items.summary: Japanese stocks experienced an optimistic week with the Nikkei 225 showing an upward trend on Friday, driven by eased expectations regarding US rate hikes and encouraging local economic indicators. The market reacted positively to a weaker US jobs report, changing investor sentiment. With rising services activity in Japan, the Topix also notched its fifth day of gains, with Rohm and Sumco standing out as key performers.

items.title: Silver futures rise to ₹2.37 lakh per kg
items.summary: Comex silver futures increased 2.17% to $62.23 per ounce

items.title: Explained: Why Tesla shares crashed 7% to record worst single-day fall in 1 year despite record Q2 sales
items.summary: Tesla shares plunged over 7% despite the EV maker reporting record second-quarter deliveries that beat Wall Street estimates. Analysts attributed the sell-off to profit booking after the stock's recent rally, with investors now focusing on Tesla's AI, robotaxi and self-driving ambitions for future growth.

items.title: Gold futures increase to ₹1,47,710/10 gm on spot demand
items.summary: Fresh positions built up by participants led to a rise in precious metal prices, according to analysts

items.title: Multibagger stock: Indiabulls share price zooms 5% to its 52-week high following stock market rally
items.summary: Indiabulls share price jumped 5% to hit its 52-week high of  ₹29.50 on Friday, July 3, outperforming the broader market rally on Dalal Street. The multibagger stock has rallied 230% from its 52-week low of  ₹8.93, which it had touched in February 2026.

items.title: Quick commerce is helping Nestle India's premium products reach beyond metros, MD Tiwary says at AGM
items.summary: Nestle India is prioritizing quick commerce for premium products and focusing on healthier reformulations, according to Chairman Manish Tiwary. The company is expanding its digital reach and improving nutritional profiles by reducing sugar, salt, and fat. Rural India is identified as a key growth driver, with investments in distribution and technology to support future demand and brand building.

items.title: Andhra Pradesh CM Chandrababu Naidu launches JSW's Rs 16,350 crore steel plant in Kadapa
items.summary: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu has inaugurated the construction of the JSW Rayalaseema Integrated Steel Plant in Kadapa district. This Rs 16,350 crore project, set to be a modern green steel facility, will be developed in two phases and aims for commercial production by March 2028. The initiative is expected to create numerous jobs and boost the state's industrial output.

items.title: Supreme Court stays Delhi government's CAG audit of power discoms
items.summary: In a significant ruling, the Supreme Court has put a stop to the Delhi government's planned inquiry into power distribution companies. This comes in light of the government's push for a Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) examination into the staggering Rs 38,500 crore accrued in Regulatory Assets, intended to be passed on to consumers. The court's decision followed input from the Delhi Electricity Regulatory Commission alongside private discom representatives.

items.title: Why is SpiceJet not operating from Chennai anymore? | Explained
items.summary: The airline’s available fleet has shrunk to around 12-13 aircraft from more than 30 a few months ago, forcing the temporary suspension of services to several destinations, including Chennai, Guwahati and Varanasi.

items.title: PM Modi to inaugurate CG Semi’s OSAT facility at Sanand on Saturday
items.summary: The facility, developed by CG Semi Pvt Ltd in partnership with Japan’s Renesas Electronics and Thailand’s Stars Microelectronics, is among the first four semiconductor projects approved under the India Semiconductor Mission

items.title: Sumitomo Chemical shares soar 11%, record biggest single-day surge in nearly 2 years. Here’s why
items.summary: Sumitomo Chemical India shares surged around 11% as heavy volumes and a major development at its Japanese parent’s Korean subsidiary lifted sentiment. Dongwoo Fine-Chem’s joint venture with Samsung Electro-Mechanics for glass core substrates boosted optimism around next-generation semiconductor packaging demand. The stock hit a fresh high amid strong trading activity.

items.title: ACME Green Molecules, Mitsubishi Gas Chemical sign $1 billion green methanol supply deal
items.summary: India's first global green marine fuel pact sees ACME Green Molecules partnering with Japan's MGC to supply 100,000 tonnes of green methanol annually, valued at $1 billion. This significant deal aims to bolster the shipping industry's shift to low-carbon alternatives, meeting stringent international environmental standards. The renewable fuel, produced in Odisha, will support the global transition to sustainable energy markets.

items.title: Insurance regulator IRDAI plans overhaul of commission rules to curb mis-selling, sources say
items.summary: India's insurance regulator is set to overhaul distributor commissions, proposing payments spread over a policy's life to curb mis-selling and reduce high distribution costs. This move aligns India with global practices and aims to ensure customer suitability over sales volume. A draft framework is expected soon, potentially impacting how agents are compensated for their services.

items.title: Can US Fed hike rates aggressively this year? How a 50 bps rate increase could impact Indian stock market
items.summary: Amid fluctuating expectations for US Federal Reserve rate hikes due to soft job data, experts highlight potential impacts on the Indian stock market. As inflation remains above target, the reactions of both the US and Indian markets could shift dramatically in the coming months.

items.title: Nifty holds above 24,350 at noon; IT, Pharma and Financials lead broad rally
items.summary: BSE market statistics painted an encouraging picture at midday. Of 4,178 stocks traded, 2,217 advanced against 1,721 declines, with 240 unchanged.

items.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism shares hit 10% upper circuit for second straight day. Newly listed stock nears its issue price
items.summary: Waterways Leisure Tourism share price surged to the upper circuit for the second session on 3 July, following a weak stock market debut. Analysts suggest sustained buying interest indicates a potential long-term opportunity, despite the stock's volatility and high P/E ratio.

items.title: VBL shares rise 1.9% after subsidiary merger announcement
items.summary: The company announced two of its South African subsidiaries  — Twizza Proprietary Limited and   Beverage Company Proprietary Limited (Bevco) — will be absorbed into its holding company

items.title: Global Market: Australian shares log best week in 3 as Fed-hike bets cool; banks, miners lead
items.summary: Indian investors found a reason to cheer as Australian shares posted their strongest week in three, driven by a global surge in commodity and financial stocks. Softer U.S. jobs data has eased concerns about further interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve, boosting market sentiment. Miners and banks, key components of the Australian market, led the gains, with gold stocks experiencing a significant uplift.

items.title: The AI trade is losing one of its key signals
items.summary: Expectations for an eventual AI bonanza could prove misplaced

items.title: South Korean shares rebound 6% but face second weekly loss
items.summary: South Korean equities rebounded sharply on Friday, with the Kospi jumping nearly 6%, led by strong gains in heavyweight stocks like Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix. The recovery came after earlier losses driven by a global tech selloff and concerns that the AI-driven rally may be peaking.

items.title: Govt lens on e-rickshaw remote shutdown issue; two apps including BAT-BMS removed from app stores
items.summary: Concerns over e-rickshaws being remotely disabled via a Chinese app, BAT-BMS, have prompted government action. IT Secretary S Krishnan confirmed two such applications were identified and promptly removed from app stores. He emphasized app stores' responsibility for due diligence and stated the government will engage with them to prevent the availability of potentially hazardous apps, addressing widespread cybersecurity worries.

items.title: Small-cap stock under  ₹50: Sindhu Trade Links up over 8%. Do you own?
items.summary: Sindhu Trade Links share price has gained 8% in one month and has rallied 28% in three months. The smallcap stock has surged 40% in six months, while it has fallen 6% in one year. Over the past five years, Sindhu Trade Links shares have delivered multibagger returns of 760%.

items.title: Delhi EV policy's real challenge for automakers could come if other states follow: Morgan Stanley
items.summary: Delhi's ambitious EV Policy 2026 aims to accelerate clean mobility by phasing out internal combustion engine vehicles in select segments, with incentives for electric adoption. While immediate impact on automakers is modest, the risk lies in similar policies spreading nationwide. The report highlights challenges for the motorcycle segment and emphasizes localized battery manufacturing for energy security.

items.title: IC Electricals IPO day 1: GMP shows 44% listing pop; price, key dates, other details of the SME IPO
items.summary: IC Electricals IPO opened for subscription on Friday, 3 July, and will close on Tuesday, 7 July. The issue has already been fully subscribed.&nbsp;

items.title: ICICI Bank mulls first benchmark dollar bond sale since 2017
items.summary: The bank could raise at least $500 million in offshore bonds

items.title: Oceans have never been this hot, and El Nino will make it worse
items.summary: Rising ocean temperatures have many consequences

items.title: Consumer & healthcare best placed most sectors stable, says Brickwork
items.summary: India's consumer goods and healthcare sectors are poised for strong growth this fiscal year, fueled by tax cuts, rising consumer demand, and a booming medical tourism market.  Rating agency Brickwork highlights robust revenue projections for consumer goods and significant opportunities in healthcare.  While most sectors show a stable outlook, the power distribution segment faces challenges due to high debt.

items.title: Why Nebius & CoreWeave stocks tumbled after Meta's AI cloud plans
Sandip Sabharwal calls IT a tactical trade, stays bullish on autos
items.summary: Market expert Sandip Sabharwal views India's IT sector as a tactical trading opportunity, not a long-term structural uptrend, despite lower valuations. He finds Avenue Supermarts' premium valuation unjustified despite respectable performance. items.title: Jewellers may be louped in for idle gold mobilisation

This initiative seeks to reduce India's reliance on imported bullion, especially with gold prices and import duties impacting jewellery demand. Discussions involving ministers, the RBI, banks, and industry representatives are underway, with an announcement anticipated before the festive season.

items.title: From being a trust to gaining trust: AIFs call for switch to LLP model

The industry advocates for converting Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) from trusts to Limited Liability Partnerships (LLPs), a globally recognized and preferred structure. This move aims to provide greater clarity on liability for foreign managers and investors, aligning India with international fund hubs.

Attrition rates are also anticipated to decrease as talent mobility slows.

items.title: India-UK trade pact sparks rush of export orders ahead of July 15 rollout

Anticipating tariff cuts, businesses in textiles, garments, leather goods, and chemicals are reporting substantial increases in bookings. This trade pact promises to boost competitiveness for Indian products, with gems and jewellery exports also poised for growth, though toy exporters face compliance hurdles.

items.title: RBI backs crypto containment and keeps ban on table, officials tell house panel
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has strongly advised a prohibition strategy for crypto assets, recommending that banks and financial institutions be completely shielded from them. Officials argued that allowing exposure could legitimize speculative products with no economic benefit and pose financial stability risks. The RBI also questioned India's top crypto adopter ranking and warned stablecoins could threaten monetary sovereignty.

items.title: India-EU FTA legal scrub to end in 10-12 days, deal by Dec 31: Piyush Goyal

items.title: Buffer stocks, imports to keep pulse prices steady

Ample government reserves, coupled with strong output from key exporting nations like Africa and Myanmar, are ensuring sufficient supply. Weak consumer demand since March has also contributed to stable pricing, offering relief to the market.

items.title: IBBI seeks tighter regulations around personal guarantors, valuation of insolvent firms

items.title: RBI tells parliamentary panel that virtual digital assets like cryptocurrency a threat to economy

While some nations have banned such assets, others have implemented strict regulations.

items.title: Agri Picks Report: Geojit
items.summary: According to Geojit, Rubber prices rose nearly 10% over the past three-four weeks and hit a record high of 140 rupees a kg due to pent-up demand after easing of the pandemic induced lockdowns amid a supply crunch.

items.title: Oil prices slip as growing stockpiles signal bumpy fuel demand recovery
items.summary: U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude futures fell 20 cents, or 0.5%, to $37.85 a barrel at 0056 GMT, after climbing 3.5% on Wednesday.

items.title: US EIA cuts 2020 world oil demand growth forecast
items.summary: In its monthly forecast, the agency cut its oil demand growth estimate for 2021 by 490,000 bpd to 6.53 million bpd.

items.title: Energy Weekly Report: Motilal Oswal
items.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Oil prices hit a rough patch last week, falling back in line with broader financial markets. Prices did try to inch higher, but experienced selling pressure at highs.

items.title: Precious Metals Weekly Report: Motilal Oswal
items.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Gold fell to the lowest level in a month primarily as the dollar rose against its major crosses and better-than-expected economic number from the US.

items.title: Base Metals Weekly Report: Motilal Oswal
items.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Base metals traded choppy last week, with prices hitting multi month highs at the start of the week and followed by some profit taking on back of volatility in the dollar and mixed data signals from China and US.

items.title: Uncle Sam is wrong, India and China are doing their bit to fight climate change
items.summary: It is true that there is an increase in carbon emission by India and China due to homegrown consumption and lack of infrastructure. But let#39;s keep in mind that India#39;s carbon emissions per capita are still 10 times lower and China#39;s is half of that compared to the US.
